provenance.author: Poly by Google
provenance.notes: Coconut palm from Poly by Google, distributed via poly.pizza/m/bXUTyfiwqBb under CC-BY (source labels it 'CC-BY'; normalized to CC-BY-4.0 here, attribution preserved). Normalized via @gltf-transform: centered to a ground-center pivot (x/z symmetric, min.y=0) and webp-compressed (2.38MB -> 329KB). Modeled fronds (not alpha planes), so the baked card silhouette is the true palm shape. Distinct kebab id 'coconut-palm' so it never collides with the legacy camelCase 'coconut' billboard namespace. Credit in THIRD-PARTY-ASSETS.md.

lod.label: mesh-near+card-far
lod.rationale: A 27m coconut palm is a sparse coastal landmark, not dense ground cover. Up close it reads as a real fronded crown on a bare trunk (cheap 2.8k-tri mesh, globally-capped near tier); past ~50m it collapses to a baked front-view alpha card visible out to 140m. A single-front card is acceptable for a radially-sparse palm; an 8x3 octahedral hero (like fan-palm/bamboo-grove) is the higher-fidelity upgrade if the flat far-card reads poorly in playtest.

bands.notes: Baked front-view alpha card (scripts/bake-veg-card.mjs); instanced + hard-culled at 140m.
